manual_title: Emergency Cardiovascular Resuscitation Pharmacology: A Technical Reference Manual

medications.name: Epinephrine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for cardiac arrest; administer undiluted as a direct rapid IV/IO push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Initial Bolus: 1 mg IV/IO over 1–2 seconds. Repeat Interval: Every 3–5 minutes during cardiac arrest. Flush: Follow with a 20 mL NS flush and elevate extremity.
critical_safety_alerts: High-dose regimens not routinely recommended.
Extravasation via peripheral line can cause severe localized tissue necrosis; switch to central line post-ROSC if continuous infusion is initiated.

medications.name: Amiodarone
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: First Bolus: Dilute 300 mg (2 vials) into 20–30 mL of D5W or NS. Second Bolus: Dilute 150 mg (1 vial) into 20–30 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: First Bolus: 300 mg IV/IO rapid push. Second Bolus: 150 mg IV/IO rapid push. Repeat Interval: Once after 3–5 minutes if VF/pVT persists.
critical_safety_alerts: Adsorption: Adheres to PVC containers; use non-PVC or glass if preparing post-ROSC maintenance infusions.
Excipient alert: Contains polysorbate 80, which can induce profound hypotension post-ROSC.

medications.name: Lidocaine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for cardiac arrest; administer undiluted as a direct rapid IV/IO push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Initial Bolus: 1.0–1.5 mg/kg IV/IO rapid push. Repeat Interval: 0.5–0.75 mg/kg every 5–10 minutes. Max Dose: 3 mg/kg total.
critical_safety_alerts: Monitor for signs of Lidocaine toxicity (neurotoxicity: slurred speech, altered mental status, muscle twitching, seizures) post-ROSC.
Reduce maintenance dosing in severe hepatic dysfunction or heart failure.

medications.name: Magnesium Sulfate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 2 g (4 mL of stock solution) into 10–20 mL of D5W or NS for rapid push in cardiac arrest.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 2 g IV/IO administered over 1–2 minutes in pulseless Torsades de Pointes.
critical_safety_alerts: Rapid administration in patients with a pulse causes profound vasodilation and hypotension.
Monitor for neuromuscular blockade, loss of deep tendon reflexes, and respiratory depression post-ROSC.

medications.name: Calcium Chloride
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for emergency resuscitation; administer undiluted as a slow IV/IO push over 2–5 minutes.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 500 mg to 1000 mg (5–10 mL) IV/IO over 2–5 minutes.
critical_safety_alerts: Incompatibility: Extensively precipitates if mixed with Sodium Bicarbonate; thoroughly flush lines between administrations.
Tissue Sclerosant: Severe tissue necrosis and sloughing if extravasated; central venous line preferred.

medications.name: Sodium Bicarbonate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for acute emergency resuscitation; administer undiluted as a slow IV/IO push over 3–5 minutes.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO slow push. Repeat Interval: 0.5 mEq/kg every 10 minutes based on arterial blood gas (ABG) and pH tracking.
critical_safety_alerts: Chemical Precipitation: Forms insoluble calcium carbonate precipitates when combined with Calcium Chloride or Calcium Gluconate.
Causes intracellular acidosis if ventilation is inadequate (shifts CO2 balance).

medications.name: Adenosine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; draw up undiluted. Prepare standard two-syringe rapid flush technique or a single syringe mixed with saline.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: First Dose: 6 mg IV rapid bolus over 1–2 seconds via proximal port, immediately followed by 20 mL NS flush. Second Dose: 12 mg IV rapid bolus after 1–2 minutes if conversion fails.
critical_safety_alerts: Transient Asystole: Warn patients of imminent, brief, distressing chest pain, dyspnea, or flushing.
Dosing Alterations: Reduce initial dose to 3 mg if administered via a central line, or if patient is taking dipridamole/carbamazepine.

medications.name: Amiodarone Infusion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Loading Dose: Dilute 150 mg (3 mL) into 100 mL of D5W. Maintenance Infusion: Dilute 900 mg (18 mL) into 500 mL of D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Loading Dose: 150 mg IV administered over 10 minutes (15 mg/min). Maintenance: 1 mg/min for the first 6 hours (33.3 mL/hr), then decelerate to 0.5 mg/min for the next 18 hours (16.6 mL/hr).
critical_safety_alerts: Hypotension/Bradycardia: Rapid loading infusions provoke severe arterial hypotension. Slow or pause infusion if BP drops.
Solvent Restriction: Incompatible with Normal Saline for long-term infusions; must use D5W and non-PVC sets.

medications.name: Procainamide
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 1000 mg (10 mL) into 100 mL or 250 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Loading Infusion: 20–50 mg/min IV continuous infusion. Max Load: 17 mg/kg. Maintenance: 1–4 mg/min continuous infusion.
critical_safety_alerts: Discontinuation Criteria: Stop loading infusion immediately if: (1) Arrythmia suppresses, (2) Hypotension ensues, (3) QRS widens by >50%, or (4) Max dose reached.
Avoid in patients with a prolonged baseline QTc interval.

medications.name: Diltiazem
clinical_indications: Rapid Ventricular Rate Control in AF / Flutter
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Direct Bolus: Administer undiluted. Continuous Infusion: Dilute 125 mg (25 mL) into 100 mL of NS or D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: First Bolus: 0.25 mg/kg actual body weight IV slow push over 2 minutes (typical dose 15–20 mg). Second Bolus: 0.35 mg/kg IV after 15 minutes if rate control is inadequate. Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 5–15 mg/hr.
critical_safety_alerts: Contraindicated in decompensated heart failure, severe LV dysfunction (EF < 40%), and Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW) syndrome with pre-excited AF.
Anticipate peripheral vasodilation and negative inotropic hypotension.

medications.name: Metoprolol Tartrate
clinical_indications: Rate Control in AF / Hyperadrenergic SVT
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted as a slow IV push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 5 mg IV slow push over 2 minutes. Repeat Interval: Every 5 minutes as needed. Max Dose: 15 mg total (3 doses).
critical_safety_alerts: Avoid in cardiogenic shock, active bronchospasm (severe asthma/COPD), advanced AV block, or decompensated heart failure.
Monitor for abrupt bradycardia or drop in cardiac output.

medications.name: Ibutilide
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: May administer undiluted, or dilute 1 mg (10 mL) into 50 mL of NS or D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Dosing (>= 60 kg): 1 mg IV infused continuously over 10 minutes. Dosing (< 60 kg): 0.01 mg/kg IV over 10 minutes. Repeat Interval: Repeat once after 10 minutes if arrhythmia persists.
critical_safety_alerts: Proarrhythmia Risk: High risk of inducing Torsades de Pointes. Continuous ECG monitoring is mandatory during infusion and for at least 4 hours post-infusion.
Correct K+ and Mg2+ levels prior to administration.

medications.name: Atropine Sulfate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted as a rapid IV push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 1 mg IV rapid push. Repeat Interval: Every 3–5 minutes as clinically indicated. Max Cumulative Dose: 3 mg total.
critical_safety_alerts: Paradoxical Bradycardia: Doses < 0.5 mg or slow administration can cause central vagal stimulation, paradoxical heart rate slowing, or advanced AV block.
Ineffective in type II second-degree or third-degree AV blocks with wide QRS complexes.

medications.name: Dopamine Infusion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Use premixed container; if compounding, add 400 mg (10 mL vial) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Chronotropic / beta-adrenergic range of 2–10 mcg/kg/min. Titrate up in increments of 2–5 mcg/kg/min based on hemodynamics.
critical_safety_alerts: Arrhythmogenic Potential: Highly tachyarrhythmogenic; can provoke ventricular ectopy and atrial fibrillation.
Extravasation risk causes localized skin necrosis; phentolamine is the antidote. Central line access preferred.

medications.name: Epinephrine Infusion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Withdraw 1 mg (1 mL) of stock and dilute into 250 mL of NS or D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: 2–10 mcg/min non-weight-based continuous titration. Adjust by 1–2 mcg/min every 3–5 minutes to achieve targeted MAP/HR.
critical_safety_alerts: Extensively increases myocardial oxygen consumption (MVO2); may induce localized subendocardial ischemia or tachyarrhythmias.
Central line access strongly preferred; monitor peripheral site closely if used emergency-short term.

medications.name: Isoproterenol
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 1 mg (5 vials) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 2 mcg/min. Titrate within a range of 2–10 mcg/min based on heart rate response.
critical_safety_alerts: Profound Vasodilation: Pure beta-agonist effects (beta 1 + beta 2) can cause marked peripheral vasodilation and a drop in diastolic blood pressure.
Contraindicated in ischemic heart disease due to the potential for severe tachycardia and ischemia.

medications.name: Etomidate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted via rapid IV push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Induction Bolus: 0.3 mg/kg IV push over 30–60 seconds. Calculated average adult dose range is 20–30 mg.
critical_safety_alerts: Hemodynamically stable, neutral induction profile.
Inhibits 11-beta-hydroxylase, causing transient adrenal suppression; use with caution in severe septic shock states.

medications.name: Ketamine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; can administer undiluted for RSI induction.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Induction Bolus: 1.5–2.0 mg/kg IV push over 60 seconds. Infusion (Sedation): 0.5–2.0 mg/kg/hr.
critical_safety_alerts: Sympathomimetic: Stimulates endogenous catecholamine release, raising blood pressure and heart rate. Ideal for shock states, but caution is warranted in severe ischemic coronary syndrome.

medications.name: Propofol
medications.available_stock_dosage_forms: 1000 mg / 100 mL Vial (10 mg/mL) Lipid emulsion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for direct infusion; administer undiluted via dedicated lines.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Induction Bolus: 1.5–2.5 mg/kg IV push. Continuous Infusion: 5–50 mcg/kg/min; titrate by 5 mcg/kg/min every 5 minutes.
critical_safety_alerts: Myocardial Depressant: Provokes peripheral venodilation and negative inotropic hypotension. Contraindicated in shock.
PRIS: Monitor triglycerides and CK if infused >48 hours at high doses (Propofol Infusion Syndrome).

medications.name: Succinylcholine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted via rapid IV push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Paralytic Bolus: 1.5 mg/kg IV rapid push. Complete neuromuscular blockade is achieved within 45–60 seconds.
critical_safety_alerts: Malignant Hyperthermia: Potential trigger.
Hyperkalemia: Contraindicated in patients with severe burns, crush injuries, or subacute denervating neuromuscular disease due to the risk of hyperkalemic cardiac arrest.

medications.name: Rocuronium Bromide
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted via rapid IV push.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Paralytic Bolus: 1.0–1.2 mg/kg IV rapid push for emergency intubation sequencing. Prolonged duration of action (45–60 mins).
critical_safety_alerts: Prolonged paralysis occurs in hepatic or renal impairment.
Ensure continuous, deep sedation is initiated immediately following paralytic administration.

medications.name: Naloxone
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 0.4 mg into 9 mL of NS to allow for careful micro-dosing titration if patient is not in cardiac arrest.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Apnea/Respiratory Depression: 0.04–0.4 mg IV/IO titrated slowly to restore adequate respiratory drive without triggering full withdrawal.
critical_safety_alerts: Acute Pulmonary Edema: Rapid, high-dose reversal can cause severe hyperadrenergic withdrawal, leading to acute non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema and severe agitation.

medications.name: Flumazenil
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted over 15–30 seconds.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Initial Bolus: 0.2 mg IV over 15 seconds. Repeat Interval: 0.3 mg after 30 seconds, then 0.5 mg every 1 minute. Max Dose: 3 mg total.
critical_safety_alerts: Seizure Risk: Can precipitate refractory, life-threatening seizures in patients with chronic benzodiazepine dependence or concurrent tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) overdose.

sections.section_note: All concentrations calculated below represent standard preparation methods. In clinical practice, high-concentration central line mixtures ('double-strength') are frequently utilized to prevent fluid overload.

medications.name: Norepinephrine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 4 mg (4 mL) into 250 mL of D5W or NS. D5W prevents oxidation of the drug.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 0.05–0.1 mcg/kg/min or 2–5 mcg/min non-weight-based. Titrate dynamically to maintain a targeted MAP >= 65 mmHg.
critical_safety_alerts: Ischemic digital or mesenteric vasospasm can occur at high doses.
Vesicant: Central line access is mandatory. If peripheral extravasation happens, infiltrate local tissue with 5–10 mg phentolamine.

medications.name: Dobutamine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 250 mg (20 mL) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Infuse at 2–20 mcg/kg/min. Titrate by 2.5 mcg/kg/min every 10 minutes based on cardiac index and tissue perfusion.
critical_safety_alerts: Tachyarrhythmias: Can prompt significant sinus tachycardia, ventricular ectopy, or rapid atrial fibrillation.
Vasodilation: Mild beta-2-mediated peripheral vasodilation may worsen hypotension if left ventricular filling pressures are inadequate.

medications.name: Milrinone
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 20 mg (20 mL) into 100 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: 0.25–0.75 mcg/kg/min maintenance rate. Maintenance titration adjustments are made slowly over hours due to its long half-life.
critical_safety_alerts: Hypotension: Accumulates in renal impairment. Severe systemic vasodilation can cause hypotension; avoid loading boluses in acute cardiogenic shock.
Requires parallel vasopressor support if MAP drops.

medications.name: Vasopressin
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 20 units (1 mL) into 100 mL of NS or D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Fixed, non-titrated adjunct rate of 0.03–0.04 units/min for septic shock vasoactive optimization.
critical_safety_alerts: Do not titrate up rapidly like catecholamines; high doses (>0.04 units/min) provoke severe myocardial ischemia, splanchnic hypoperfusion, and digital necrosis.

medications.name: Phenylephrine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 10 mg (1 mL) into 250 mL of NS or D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 0.5–2.0 mcg/kg/min or 40–100 mcg/min non-weight-based. Titrate to target MAP.
critical_safety_alerts: Reflex Bradycardia: Pure alpha-1 agonist effects lead to a rise in systemic vascular resistance, which can trigger a vagally-mediated drop in heart rate.
Reduces stroke volume in patients with pre-existing left ventricular dysfunction.

medications.name: Nitroglycerin
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 50 mg (10 mL) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 5–10 mcg/min. Titrate up by 5–10 mcg/min every 3–5 minutes based on chest pain level and systemic blood pressure parameters.
critical_safety_alerts: PDE-5 Inhibitor Contradiction: Do not administer if sildenafil, vardenafil, or tadalafil was used within 24–48 hours due to the risk of profound, refractory hypotension.
Adsorption: Must be prepared in non-PVC glass or polyolefin infusion containers.

medications.name: Labetalol HCl
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Bolus: Administer undiluted. Continuous Infusion: Dilute 200 mg (40 mL) into 160 mL of D5W or NS.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Intermittent Bolus: 20 mg IV slow push over 2 minutes. Repeat with escalating doses (40 mg, then 80 mg) every 10 minutes as needed (Max 300 mg). Continuous Infusion: 1–8 mg/min titrated to target blood pressure.
critical_safety_alerts: Dual alpha/beta blocker (1:7 IV ratio).
Avoid in decompensated heart failure, severe reactive airway disease, advanced heart block, or bradycardia.
Monitor for unexpected precipitous drops in cardiac output.

medications.name: Nicardipine HCl
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 25 mg (10 mL) into 240 mL of NS or D5W.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 5 mg/hr. Titrate up by 2.5 mg/hr every 5–15 minutes to a maximum rate of 15 mg/hr until target BP is achieved.
critical_safety_alerts: Phlebitis: Highly irritating to peripheral veins; rotate peripheral access sites every 12 hours or switch to central venous delivery if long-term support is required.
Avoid in advanced aortic stenosis.

medications.name: Sodium Nitroprusside
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 50 mg (2 mL) into 250 mL of D5W only.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 0.3 mcg/kg/min. Titrate by 0.5 mcg/kg/min every 5 minutes based on real-time intra-arterial line tracking (Max 10 mcg/kg/min).
critical_safety_alerts: Cyanide/Thiocyanate Toxicity: Metabolized to cyanide. Toxicity presents as unexplained metabolic acidosis, altered mental status, and lactate elevation; monitor closely if used >48 hours or in renal impairment.
Light Sensitivity: Wrap the infusion bag in light-protective foil.

medications.name: Esmolol HCl
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for the 10 mg/mL commercial vial formulation; can run directly as an infusion.
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Loading Bolus: Optional 500 mcg/kg IV push over 1 minute. Continuous Infusion: 50–300 mcg/kg/min; titrate by 50 mcg/kg/min every 5 minutes based on target heart rate and blood pressure parameters.
critical_safety_alerts: Ultra-Short Acting: Half-life is roughly 9 minutes. If severe hypotension or bradycardia occurs, turning off the infusion results in rapid clinical reversal within minutes.
Highly accurate for testing beta-blocker tolerance.

cross_references: For specific multi-center heart failure outcome registries and tracking protocols, see details referenced in the data framework under THESUS-HF II protocols.

For automated clinical calculators managing weight-based dose optimization software systems, consult code frameworks labeled under the reference files "watermarked_img_15605539625956534618.png", "watermarked_img_13413688051120390643.png", and "watermarked_img_8095023707865201722.png". Ensure concentration variables map directly to the calculated outputs detailed in Sections 3, 5, and 6 above.

medications.name: General Infusion Formula
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Use the formula: Rate (mL/hr) = [Dose (mcg/kg/min) * Weight (kg) * 60] / Concentration (mcg/mL).
medications.available_stock_dosage_forms: Standardized concentrations: Norepinephrine (16 mcg/mL), Dopamine (1600 mcg/mL), Dobutamine (1000 mcg/mL).
critical_safety_alerts: Always verify drug concentration and pump settings before starting infusion.
When using gravity drip sets (if pumps are unavailable), use the formula: Drops/min = [Volume (mL) * Drop Factor (gtts/mL)] / Time (min). Standard macro-drip factor is 10 or 15 gtts/mL.
